definitions: A high-ranking official in the Chinese imperial bureaucracy.
A public official, especially one of influence.
A small, edible citrus fruit with a loose skin, typically reddish-orange.
The standard literary and official form of Chinese, based on the Beijing dialect, and spoken by the largest group of Chinese people.
examples: The emperor appointed a new Mandarin to the court.
She peeled a Mandarin for a snack.
The Mandarins of the financial world met to discuss policy.
